description_long: This is a large, narrow-body commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage and a pair of swept-back, low-mounted wings. The aircraft features three rear-mounted jet engines, two on either side of the fuselage and one integrated into the tail, which is a distinctive T-tail configuration. The nose is rounded and tapers smoothly into the cockpit area, which is positioned at the very front of the fuselage and features a row of cockpit windows. The main landing gear is housed under the wings and fuselage, and the airplane has a tricycle landing gear arrangement. The exterior is primarily a light gray or white color, with minimal visible markings or livery. The overall design suggests a focus on efficient aerodynamics and passenger transport, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century commercial jets.
description: A large, narrow-body commercial jet airliner with three rear-mounted engines, swept low wings, and a T-tail, finished in light gray or white.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the T-tail, three rear-mounted engines, and the full span of the swept-back wings.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, low-mounted swept wings, and the forward cockpit windows.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical layout of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the T-tail and rear engine configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet engines, two on the fuselage sides and one integrated into the tail.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
